Key binding interactions include ion–dipole between the host carbonyl/sulfonate groups and the guest trimethylammonium (Kme3) and guanidinium (Arg) cations, hydrophobic encapsulation of aliphatic side chains and the Tyr aromatic ring, and hydrogen bonding with the peptide backbone.</answer>

The approximate cavity size of CB[7] is ~5.5 Å diameter, CB[8] ~6.9 Å diameter, both with heights of ~9.1 Å, well-suited to encapsulate the quinoline and epoxyquinone moieties.
